scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. Source of truth: the official Pipedrive API v1 OpenAPI document (fetched from https://developers.pipedrive.com/docs/api/v1/openapi.yaml), cross-checked against the live developers.pipedrive.com v1 endpoint-reference pages. IMPORTANT FINDING during Pass B research: Pipedrive migrated full CRUD (list/get/create/update/delete) for Deals, Persons, Organizations, Products, Activities, Pipelines, and Stages to API v2 — the v1 OpenAPI document (and the live v1 docs pages for those resources, which now redirect their 'Get all'/'Add a new' operations to /api/v2/<resource>) no longer documents plain /deals, /persons, /organizations, /products, /activities, /pipelines, or /stages CRUD at all; only each resource's v1-exclusive sub-resource endpoints (changelog, files, followers, merge, mailMessages, permittedUsers, etc.) remain under v1. This bundle's 6 original streams keep reading the v1 list endpoints unchanged (Pipedrive still serves them for backward compatibility, matching legacy exactly) — migrating them to v2 is a deliberate API-version change out of scope for a parity-preserving Pass B expansion, not attempted here (see docs.md Known limits). Pass B instead adds 14 new streams and 17 write actions from resources that remain genuinely v1-native with full CRUD: notes, leads, saved filters, custom activity types, lead labels, webhooks, plus 10 read-only reference/metadata streams (deal/person/organization/product/lead fields, roles, legacy teams, lead sources, currencies). Every endpoint below is covered_by a stream/write action XOR excluded with a real category and reason.

excluded.reason: single-note detail; identical record shape to the notes list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
per-note comment thread sub-resource, a fine-grained edit surface on an already-covered note rather than an independent business object; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
adds a comment to one note's comment thread; narrower than the note-level create/update/delete actions already covered
single-comment detail on a note's comment thread; same out-of-scope reasoning as the parent comments list
updates one comment on a note's comment thread; same out-of-scope reasoning as the parent comments list
deletes one comment on a note's comment thread; same out-of-scope reasoning as the parent comments list

note fields are a fixed, non-customizable field set (content/pinning flags only, no user-defined custom fields); the metadata this endpoint returns has no independent sync value beyond what notes.json's schema already documents
archived-leads-only view of the same Lead resource already covered by the leads stream; a narrower filtered variant, not an independent business object
full-text search over leads by term; the leads stream already provides complete list coverage, this is a query convenience endpoint layered on the same data
single-lead detail; identical record shape to the leads list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
returns the list of user ids permitted to access one lead (an access-control check), not a syncable business record
creates a new custom deal field definition; schema/metadata-authoring mutation, out of scope for this pass (the field-definition streams are read-only reference data in this bundle)
bulk delete of multiple deal-field definitions by an id-list request body; deleting a field definition removes its data from every deal, no bulk-destructive action added
single-deal-field detail; identical record shape to the deal_fields list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
irreversibly deletes a custom field definition and its data from every deal; out of scope for this pass, no safer covered alternative
updates a custom deal field's definition (name/options); schema-authoring mutation, out of scope for this pass
creates a new custom person field definition; same schema-authoring-mutation reasoning as dealFields' POST
bulk delete of multiple person-field definitions by an id-list request body; same reasoning as dealFields' bulk delete
single-person-field detail; identical record shape to the person_fields list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
irreversibly deletes a custom field definition and its data from every person; out of scope for this pass
updates a custom person field's definition; schema-authoring mutation, out of scope for this pass
creates a new custom organization field definition; same schema-authoring-mutation reasoning as dealFields' POST
bulk delete of multiple organization-field definitions by an id-list request body; same reasoning as dealFields' bulk delete
single-organization-field detail; identical record shape to the organization_fields list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
irreversibly deletes a custom field definition and its data from every organization; out of scope for this pass
updates a custom organization field's definition; schema-authoring mutation, out of scope for this pass
creates a new custom product field definition; same schema-authoring-mutation reasoning as dealFields' POST
bulk delete of multiple product-field definitions by an id-list request body; same reasoning as dealFields' bulk delete
single-product-field detail; identical record shape to the product_fields list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
irreversibly deletes a custom field definition and its data from every product; out of scope for this pass
updates a custom product field's definition; schema-authoring mutation, out of scope for this pass
activity fields are a largely fixed built-in field set with limited custom-field usage compared to deal/person/organization/product fields; deprioritized in this pass's field-metadata coverage
bulk delete of multiple saved filters by an id-list request body; delete_filter already covers the safer per-filter equivalent
returns the field/condition-operator vocabulary usable when building a filter's conditions object; a schema-discovery helper, not a syncable business record
single-filter detail; identical record shape to the filters list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
creates a new legacy team; Pipedrive documents this resource as 'legacy' in favor of the newer role/permission-set model, deprioritized for write coverage in this pass
single-team detail; identical record shape to the legacy_teams list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
updates a legacy team's name/manager; same legacy-resource deprioritization as its create counterpart
per-team user-membership sub-list; the legacy_teams stream's own users[] field already carries this same membership data
adds users to a legacy team's membership; fine-grained membership edit of an already-covered parent record
removes users from a legacy team's membership; same fine-grained-edit reasoning as the add-users endpoint
reverse lookup of which teams one user belongs to; a query convenience over data the legacy_teams stream's users[] field already exposes
creates a new permission role; account-permission-structure authoring, out of scope for this pass (the roles stream is read-only reference data in this bundle)
single-role detail; identical record shape to the roles list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
updates a permission role's name/parent; account-permission-structure authoring, out of scope for this pass
irreversibly deletes a permission role affecting every assigned user's access; out of scope for this pass, no safer covered alternative
per-role user-assignment sub-list; a narrower fine-grained view of account permission structure than the roles stream itself
assigns a user to a role; account-permission-structure mutation, out of scope for this pass
removes a user's role assignment, altering their account permissions; out of scope for this pass
per-role visibility/access settings sub-resource; a narrower fine-grained view of account permission structure than the roles stream itself
sets a per-role visibility/access setting; account-permission-structure mutation, out of scope for this pass
per-role pipeline-visibility sub-resource; a narrower fine-grained view of account permission structure than the roles stream itself
sets which pipelines a role can see; account-permission-structure mutation, out of scope for this pass
Pipedrive's newer fixed (non-editable) permission-set catalog, a read-only complement to the roles resource already covered; deprioritized for this pass's breadth-vs-cost triage
single-permission-set detail; same out-of-scope reasoning as the parent list endpoint, which itself was deprioritized
per-permission-set user-assignment sub-list; same out-of-scope reasoning as permissionSets itself
returns the calling user's own account-level UI/feature-flag settings, a single caller-scoped configuration object rather than a syncable multi-record business resource
lists the calling user's connected third-party service accounts (email, calendar, etc.); an integration-configuration artifact, not CRM business data
name-search convenience lookup over the same Users resource already covered by the users stream
returns the calling user's own record; identical shape to one item of the users list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-user detail; identical record shape to the users list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
updates a user's account settings (active state, admin flag); account-administration mutation, out of scope for this pass
invites/creates a new Pipedrive account user; account-provisioning mutation, out of scope for this pass
per-user follower sub-list, a fine-grained edit-surface view rather than an independent business object
per-user effective-permissions computed view; account-permission-structure data, out of scope for this pass
per-user role-assignment sub-list; same account-permission-structure reasoning as roles/{id}/assignments
per-user effective role-settings computed view; account-permission-structure data, out of scope for this pass

archived-deals-only filtered view of the same Deal resource the deals stream already reads; a narrower variant, not an independent object, and shares the deals stream's v1-read-only status (see scope note)
aggregated deal-value/count rollup, a computed reporting summary rather than an independently syncable business object
aggregated rollup over archived deals; same computed-reporting reasoning as deals/summary
aggregated deal-count-by-time-period rollup for chart rendering, a computed reporting view rather than an independently syncable business object
same computed-reporting reasoning as deals/timeline, scoped to archived deals
per-deal field-value change history, a fine-grained audit sub-resource of an already-covered deal rather than an independent business object
clones an existing deal server-side; a convenience action, not a data-shape create_deal write this pass models (the deals resource's create/update/delete live on v2, out of scope per the scope note)
per-deal attached-file sub-list; the top-level /files resource (see Files exclusions below) covers the same file metadata more generally, and file binary payloads are out of scope
chronological activity-log feed for one deal (a UI timeline rendering), not a stable syncable business record
per-deal follower sub-list, a fine-grained edit-surface view rather than an independent business object
adds a follower to one deal; fine-grained edit of an already-covered parent record
removes a follower from one deal; same fine-grained-edit reasoning as the add-follower endpoint
per-deal linked-email sub-list; email content/threading is a separate Mailbox feature area out of scope for this pass
merges two deals into one, an irreversible structural operation rather than an ordinary field-level update; out of scope for this pass
per-deal participant (linked-person) sub-list; a fine-grained association view rather than an independent business object
adds a participant to one deal; fine-grained edit of an already-covered parent record
removes a participant from one deal; same fine-grained-edit reasoning as the add-participant endpoint
per-deal participant-change audit history; same audit-sub-resource reasoning as deals/{id}/changelog
returns the list of user ids permitted to access one deal (an access-control check), not a syncable business record
per-organization field-value change history; same audit-sub-resource reasoning as deals/{id}/changelog
per-organization attached-file sub-list; same file-metadata reasoning as deals/{id}/files
chronological activity-log feed for one organization; same UI-timeline reasoning as deals/{id}/flow
per-organization follower sub-list; same fine-grained-edit-surface reasoning as deals/{id}/followers
adds a follower to one organization; fine-grained edit of an already-covered parent record
removes a follower from one organization; same fine-grained-edit reasoning as the add-follower endpoint
per-organization linked-email sub-list; same Mailbox-feature-area reasoning as deals/{id}/mailMessages
merges two organizations into one, an irreversible structural operation; same reasoning as deals/{id}/merge
access-control check for one organization; same reasoning as deals/{id}/permittedUsers
per-person field-value change history; same audit-sub-resource reasoning as deals/{id}/changelog
per-person attached-file sub-list; same file-metadata reasoning as deals/{id}/files
chronological activity-log feed for one person; same UI-timeline reasoning as deals/{id}/flow
per-person follower sub-list; same fine-grained-edit-surface reasoning as deals/{id}/followers

removes a follower from one person; same fine-grained-edit reasoning as the add-follower endpoint
per-person linked-email sub-list; same Mailbox-feature-area reasoning as deals/{id}/mailMessages
merges two persons into one, an irreversible structural operation; same reasoning as deals/{id}/merge
access-control check for one person; same reasoning as deals/{id}/permittedUsers
removes a person's profile picture image; binary-image management, out of scope for a record-sync connector
uploads a person's profile picture image (multipart file upload); binary-image management, out of scope for a record-sync connector
per-person linked-products sub-list; a fine-grained association view rather than an independent business object
per-product linked-deals sub-list; a fine-grained association view rather than an independent business object, the deals stream already covers deal records themselves
per-product attached-file sub-list; same file-metadata reasoning as deals/{id}/files
per-product follower sub-list; same fine-grained-edit-surface reasoning as deals/{id}/followers
adds a follower to one product; fine-grained edit of an already-covered parent record
removes a follower from one product; same fine-grained-edit reasoning as the add-follower endpoint
access-control check for one product; same reasoning as deals/{id}/permittedUsers
computed deal-conversion-rate rollup for one pipeline, a reporting aggregate rather than an independently syncable business object
per-pipeline linked-deals sub-list; the pipeline resource itself is v2-only (see scope note), out of scope alongside it
computed deal-stage-movement rollup for one pipeline; same reporting-aggregate reasoning as conversion_statistics

file metadata list whose only useful payload (the file content) is fetched via a separate binary-download endpoint; binary-attachment management is out of scope for a record-sync connector
uploads a new file attachment (multipart file upload); binary-attachment management, out of scope
creates a file reference to an external cloud-storage document; binary-attachment management, out of scope
links an existing remote file to an item; binary-attachment management, out of scope
deletes a file attachment; binary-attachment management, out of scope
single-file metadata detail; binary-attachment management, out of scope
updates a file's name/description; binary-attachment management, out of scope
downloads the raw binary file content; explicitly a binary payload, out of scope
call-log records tied to Pipedrive's telephony/VoIP integration feature, a niche add-on most accounts do not use; deprioritized for this pass
creates a call-log entry; same telephony-integration-feature deprioritization as the list endpoint
single-call-log detail; same out-of-scope reasoning as the parent list endpoint, which itself was deprioritized
deletes a call-log entry; same telephony-integration-feature deprioritization
attaches a binary audio recording file to a call log; binary-attachment management, out of scope
registers a messaging-app integration channel (Pipedrive's Messaging Apps Extension feature), a developer-platform integration primitive rather than CRM business data
webhook-style inbound-message receiver for a registered messaging channel; a push-delivery endpoint, not a syncable record read

single linked-email-message detail; Pipedrive's Mailbox email-sync feature is a separate integration surface with its own OAuth-email-provider prerequisites, out of scope for this pass
linked-email-thread list; same Mailbox-feature-area deprioritization

links a user's video-call-provider account for the Meetings scheduling feature; an integration-configuration mutation, not CRM business data

requires a REQUIRED org_id query parameter (listable only per-organization, not as a standalone top-level collection); expressing this would require a fan_out driven by the organizations stream's ids, deprioritized for this pass
creates a relationship between two organizations; deprioritized alongside the list endpoint's fan_out requirement

a UI-facing 'recently viewed/changed across all object types' activity feed, not a stable single-resource-typed business record
the company's own Pipedrive subscription/billing add-on list, account-billing metadata rather than CRM business data
